message.content:
{
  "applicable": true,
  "component_key": "reader-sources",
  "title": "Füge memU als neuen Feed-Typ hinzu",
  "motivation": "memU bietet ein Dateisystem-als-Gedächtnis, das als persistente, strukturierte Wissensquelle für Joni dienen kann, indem es Dateien als Erinnerungen speichert.",
  "desired": "Erstelle in reader.py/sources.py einen memU-Feed-Adapter, der ein memU-Dateisystemverzeichnis einliest und den Inhalt als abrufbare Informationen für Joni bereitstellt.",
  "acceptance": "Joni kann auf ein konfiguriertes memU-Verzeichnis zugreifen und dessen Inhalte als Antworten auf Anfragen nachweisen (z. B. durch eine Testabfrage, die ein gespeichertes Faktum zurückgibt)."
}
message.reasoning_content:
We are asked: "Could this source concretely improve exactly one of those modules without touching the protected core?" The source is the openclaw repository "memU" with description "File System as Memory, Memory Shapes the Agent". We need to evaluate if the paper/software describes a method, technique, or tool that maps onto one of the allowlisted modules. We must be strict and grounded: only say yes if the source describes something that genuinely helps one listed module, without inventing capabilities.

We have the abstract/description: "File System as Memory, Memory Shapes the Agent". It appears to be about using a file system as a memory system for an agent, where the memory shapes the agent. Possibly this is about storing memories as files in a directory structure, enabling persistent, structured memory for an agent. This could relate to the "reader-sources" module (reading layer, which feeds Joni can draw on) because it might provide a new type of source: file system-based memory. Or it could relate to "emergence" (how candidates earn development) if the memory influences strengthening. But we must see if it concretely improves one module.

We are instructed: "Output ONLY a JSON object." Options: 
{"applicable": true, "component_key": <EXACTLY one key from the allowlist>, "title": <short German imperative order>, "motivation": <why, grounded in the source, German>, "desired": <the concrete non-core change to that module, German>, "acceptance": <a measurable acceptance criterion, German>} OR {"applicable": false}.

We need to check if the memU source provides something concrete. The URL is given: https://github.com/NevaMind-AI/memU. As an AI, I can't browse the internet, but I can infer from the description. It says "File System as Memory, Memory Shapes the Agent". Likely it's a Python library or framework that uses the file system to store and retrieve agent memories. So it offers a method for memory management. Could this improve reader-sources? Reader-sources is "the reading layer (sources.py / reader.py) - which feeds Joni can draw on". If memU provides a new type of external feed (like a file system memory that the agent can read), then yes, it could be integrated as a new source. But we need to ensure it doesn't touch the protected core. Adding a new feed source to reader-sources is a non-core change because reader-sources is in the allowlist. So it's plausible.

Alternatively, could it improve emergence? If memory shapes the agent, then the agent's development (emergence) could be influenced by file system memory. But the module "emergence" is about "how candidates earn development". If memU provides a mechanism to strengthen candidates based on memory files, that might be a concrete improvement. However, the description only says "Memory Shapes the Agent", which is vague. Without more details, we need to be strict: only say yes when the source actually describes a method, technique or tool that maps onto a listed module. We don't know the internals of memU. But the title suggests it's about using file system as memory for an agent, possibly a persistent store. That might be more of a memory store than a method that would directly improve one of Joni's modules, unless Joni currently lacks such a memory and could benefit from it as a source. But Joni's reader-sources module is about what feeds Joni can draw on. Could memU provide a new feed? It might allow Joni to read from a file system memory store as a source of information, which would be a new source. So it maps onto reader-sources.

We must ground it: the source is memU, which provides a "File System as Memory" system. That is a tool that could be used to create a file-based memory store. For Joni, we could add a new reader source that reads from such a memory. That's a concrete change: adding a new source to the reading layer. The motivation: memU allows persistent, structured memory stored as files, which could serve as a long-term memory source for Joni. So applicable.

But we must be careful: the module "reader-sources" includes "which feeds Joni can draw on". So yes, a new feed type. So I'll go with that.

However, could it be that memU is more about shaping the agent itself, i.e., modifying its core behavior? The description "Memory Shapes the Agent" suggests that the memory influences the agent's decisions. That might be closer to the core, but we cannot target core. Yet the module "emergence" is about strengthening candidates, which is non-core. Could the memory be used to inform the strengthening of candidates? If memU provides a way to retrieve relevant memories that can evaluate candidate merit, that could improve emergence. But without more info, it's speculative. The most direct mapping is to reader-sources: a file system memory can be a feed of information that the agent can consult.

I'll check the allowlist: reader-sources is the reading layer.
